findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "memory_set" references sensitive artifacts (secret). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Agent Memory: durable key-value storage that survives between agent runs. Write a JSON value (16KB max) under a key. First write to a namespace claims it with y…

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "crypto_contract" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: Contract decoder: pass a verified 0x contract address to get its function ABI, or a 0x-selector (8 hex) to decode which function it is. Send { input }. Agents u…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
